content.full_text:
Thomas M. Connolly, III
Candidate State-Assembly, 77th A.D.                                    619-440-6458

                                                                    Campaign Director
                                                                    Duane K. Brown

Dear Wayne,

A few our globe Conversation the A.M., I have had
problems on juggling small bills of the campaign.

How said volunteers can be recruited up to 700 within 45 days,
as a petty cash fund of 150 for bills not exceeding 400.

A letter from the Sacramento office stating these two
rules well solve one problems.

In campaigns for either parties with 35% registrations,
is not going to draw hard huge campaign funds on their
treasures or campaign lists. But volunteers are hard
enough to come by, with out being hassled about
trivial matters.

John, I know, we care, what problems exist with
the local contact; such a letter will square two
separate hassels; and many campaigns have begs people
as treasurer and candidate that can't even sign two party
check on a day order for less than 400.

Thank you for responding to one needs.


                                        Yours Truly,
                                        Duane K. Brown

1854 Granite Hills Drive, El Cajon, California 92019

sections.question_synthetic: The sender is requesting written confirmation from the FPPC Sacramento office regarding volunteer reimbursement rules (up to $100 within 45 days) and petty cash fund procedures (up to $100 for bills not exceeding $100) to address campaign payment issues.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal question or conclusion sections present. The sender references a prior phone conversation with someone named Wayne and requests written confirmation of guidance already provided verbally. Document appears to be the outgoing letter from the campaign, not the FPPC's response letter.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender is requesting written confirmation from the FPPC Sacramento office regarding volunteer reimbursement rules (up to $100 within 45 days) and petty cash fund procedures (up to $100 for bills not exceeding $100) to address campaign payment issues.
